nervous laughter escaped her lips as she read the email from Dr. Hales on his birthday - a day that should have been about celebration and joy but felt like an ominous prelude to something far more sinister than just another year passing by. EXPERIMENT DETAILS AND RESULTS ANALYSIS
===============================

Abstract
----------
This study aimed to understand the impact of refresh frequency on user engagement within a home feed environment, using dwell time and session count as primary metrics. The experiment was conducted with 10 participants (n=10), each assigned randomly to either control or treatment groups without prior exposure bias. Results indicated that while both conditions showed similar levels of average dwell times per visit at approximately 38 seconds for the control group, a slight increase in time by about half a second when compared to the treatment condition was observed (control: M=38.2s; SD=10.5s vs. treatment: M=39.1s; SD=11.4s). Additionally, session counts per user were nearly identical across both groups with control at 3.80 and treatment also recording a count of 3.76 sessions (SD = .2), suggesting that refresh frequency did not significantly affect the number of visits within this experimental setting.
